Arkansas · State Licensing Reference

Occupational Therapist
Occupational Therapist Registered (OTR) Guide

Issued by the Arkansas State Medical Board — OT Section under the Arkansas Department of Health. Data verified for 2026.

Visit Official Board Portal
Fees Board Contact License Types How to Apply Documents Reciprocity FAQ
Total Initial Fee
application/license fee
Renewal
Every 2 years
Processing Time
4 to 6 Weeks
Subject to board volume
License Duration
2 Years
From date of issuance
Board Contact

Credential Categories

Arkansas Occupational Therapist License Types

Occupational Therapist Registered (OTR)

The standard credential to practice occupational therapy independently in Arkansas.

Certified Occupational Therapy Assistant (COTA)

Requires an associate degree and practicing under the supervision of a licensed OTR.

Application Process

How to Apply for a Arkansas Occupational Therapist Registered (OTR)

Complete Education & Fieldwork

Earn a master's or doctoral degree in occupational therapy from an ACOTE-accredited program and complete specific fieldwork requirements.

Pass the NBCOT Examination

Pass the National Board for Certification in Occupational Therapy (NBCOT) exam for OTRs.

Submit Application and Fees

Complete the state application, undergo a background check if required, and submit the necessary licensing fees.

Required Documents

Application Document Checklist

  • Official TranscriptsSent directly from your educational institution.
  • NBCOT Score ReportOfficial verification of passing the NBCOT exam.
Practice Laws

Provisional Practice & Reciprocity

Provisional Rules

Many states offer a limited permit or provisional license for new graduates waiting to take the NBCOT exam. Check specific board rules for supervisory requirements.

Reciprocity & Endorsement

Occupational therapists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current NBCOT certification.

Frequently Asked Questions

Arkansas Occupational Therapist FAQ

How much does a Arkansas OT license cost?

The initial application and licensing fee varies, often around to . Check the specific fee breakdown.

Is NBCOT certification required?

Yes, passing the NBCOT exam is a standard requirement for initial licensure across all states.
